contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 85
- Description: Security dashboard end-to-end: AWS inventory, TLS, clickable repos, scanner fixes
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/Cypher-Defense/pull/23
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/Cypher-Defense/pull/26
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/Cypher-Defense/pull/27
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/172
  
  Details:
  Got the full security dashboard data pipeline working. Fixed five Cypher-Defense scanner bugs: TLS cert parsing (RDN tuple flatten), wrong Mirim Bahia hostname (now tribomirimbahia.truesight.me), secret-scanning/dependabot reading a non-existent endpoint, an AWS scan crash sorting mixed int/string open-ports, and the core architecture bug where the compile step re-ran every scanner without credentials (consolidated to one compile step carrying the AWS keys + OPS PAT). Set all four AWS credential secrets from local working keys. Result: AWS inventory now populates (nelanco 17 instances, explorya 3), all 8 TLS checks pass, repos list. On the truesight.me dashboard: made it tolerant of the renamed JSON keys, fixed the squished GitHub table (it was confined to one CSS-grid cell; now spans full width), and made repo names link to their GitHub URL. Remaining infra items flagged for the operator: OPS PAT needs Administration:read for branch-protection/secret-scanning, and the score calculator needs key fix plus calibration.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 141.67
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
